title: Listera ovata lusus trifoliata
taxonomy: Listera ovata lusus trifoliata is a taxonomic variant of the orchid species Listera ovata. It is considered to be a subspecies or a morphological variation within the Listera ovata species. The classification of Listera ovata lusus trifoliata is not universally agreed upon and may vary depending on the source. Listera ovata lusus trifoliata is often treated as a synonym of Listera ovata, but some sources may consider it to be a distinct subspecies. The classification of this variant is still a subject of ongoing research and debate in the botanical community.

intro: Listera ovata lusus trifoliata, also known as the adder's tongue, is a rare and unusual orchid species that is a variant of the common adder's tongue (Listera ovata). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in reducing pain and promoting wound healing. Bioactive constituents found in Listera ovata lusus trifoliata include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
